title: Build and Ship a Production Static Site Generator with Python 3.14
description: Python, object-oriented programming, Git, and responsive-web learners who need to design, build, test, package, deploy, migrate, and defend a production static-site generator rather than copy a narrow Markdown replacement tutorial. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; static delivery and url models; import-safe cli and atomic pipeline; pathlib content admission; unicode and typed metadata; source-mapped asts; bounded inline and block parsing; recursion and recovery; semantic context-safe html rendering; jinja 3.1.6; slugs, permalinks, redirects, navigation, taxonomies, feeds, and sitemaps; content-addressed assets; dependency-aware incremental builds; wcag 2.2; responsive progressive enhancement; security; layered tests and validation; diagnostics and profiles; python packaging; reproducible ci and github pages deployment; migration, rollback, recovery, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate a deliberately bounded educational markdown subset compared against commonmark 0.31.2 and markdown-it-py 4.2.0, with honest conformance, raw-html, parser-complexity, browser, filesystem, template, validator, deployment, and hosting transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic publication deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-site defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves reader outcome, content authority, revision, Python and tool versions, source and route identity, trust policy, accessibility requirement, bounded-resource rule, changed fixture, failure evidence, recovery, and explicit browser-versus-production limits before adding one publishing boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; those require controlled transfer evidence.

Passing work requires a stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate parse or graph state, exact observable output, a changed and rejected case, a test that detects a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder evidence, and a named owner for remaining risk.

competencies.statement: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
misconceptions: Generating an index page is a complete reader outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.

competencies.statement: Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
misconceptions: A static site has no runtime, trust, or operational boundaries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.

competencies.statement: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
misconceptions: Generated output can be committed safely without first identifying its source and owner.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.

competencies.statement: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.
misconceptions: Accessibility can be added to templates after content and routes are finished.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.

competencies.statement: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.
misconceptions: A visually correct local page proves the published site is complete.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

competencies.statement: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.
misconceptions: Static hosting means the browser reads repository files directly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.

competencies.statement: Keep platform filesystem paths, POSIX publication paths, percent-encoded URL paths, queries, and fragments as distinct types.
misconceptions: Path joining and URL joining obey the same rules.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep platform filesystem paths, POSIX publication paths, percent-encoded URL paths, queries, and fragments as distinct types.

competencies.statement: Model root, project-subpath, preview, custom-domain, trailing-slash, and relative-link behavior under one explicit base URL.

competencies.statement: Define clean URLs, directory indexes, canonical output files, redirects, not-found behavior, and host-specific limits.

competencies.statement: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

competencies.statement: Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.

competencies.statement: Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.

competencies.statement: Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.

competencies.statement: Keep deterministic transformations pure and pass filesystem, clock, environment, logging, and publication effects through narrow adapters.

competencies.statement: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

competencies.statement: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.

competencies.statement: Declare roots, extensions, ignores, hidden files, drafts, ordering, maximum files, depth, and total bytes before traversal.

competencies.statement: Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.

competencies.statement: Detect case, Unicode, normalization, index, extension, and slug collisions before any output write.

competencies.statement: Record stable source identity, relative path, size, digest purpose, route candidate, status, and rejection reason.

competencies.statement: Choose normalization, identifier, comparison, sorting, and display policies while retaining original author text.

competencies.statement: Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.

competencies.statement: Validate allowed keys, required values, types, formats, unknown fields, duplicates, defaults, and source locations.

competencies.statement: Represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable IDs without locale ambiguity.

competencies.statement: Distinguish scanning, tokenization, block parsing, inline parsing, tree construction, transformation, validation, and rendering.

competencies.statement: Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.

competencies.statement: Retain source path, line, column, byte or code-point span, and original excerpt through transformations.

competencies.statement: Reject cycles, shared mutable children, impossible parent-child pairs, invalid spans, and unbounded depth.

competencies.statement: Traverse and transform nodes without mutating shared input or losing identity, order, diagnostics, and provenance.

competencies.statement: Advance one cursor through text, escapes, entities, code spans, delimiters, links, and literal fallbacks without dropping characters.

competencies.statement: Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.

competencies.statement: Classify delimiter runs from surrounding characters, track opener and closer state, and resolve nested emphasis deterministically.

competencies.statement: Distinguish source escapes, character references, literal text, and later HTML output encoding.

competencies.statement: Bound input length, delimiter work, nesting, diagnostics, and worst-case time with changed adversarial fixtures.

competencies.statement: Parse labels, destinations, optional titles, nesting, escaping, angle forms, and balanced delimiters under a named subset.

competencies.statement: Normalize reference labels, detect duplicates, resolve forward references, preserve unresolved source, and report locations.

competencies.statement: Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.

competencies.statement: Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.

competencies.statement: Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.

competencies.statement: Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.
misconceptions: A line starting with a hash is always a heading.

competencies.statement: Track list marker, start, indentation, tightness, continuation, quote depth, and blank lines through nested containers.

competencies.statement: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.
misconceptions: Any three backticks close every fenced code block.

competencies.statement: Define base case, recursive reduction, returned state, source advancement, and invariant for nested document structures.
misconceptions: Recursive code is correct when it eventually stops on familiar fixtures.

competencies.statement: Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.

competencies.statement: Reject or recover from excessive nesting before Python recursion, memory, or diagnostic output becomes unbounded.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject or recover from excessive nesting before Python recursion, memory, or diagnostic output becomes unbounded.

competencies.statement: Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.
misconceptions: A parser must either accept the whole file or produce no page.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.

competencies.statement: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.
misconceptions: One deeply nested happy path covers container interactions.

competencies.statement: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
misconceptions: Any HTML that looks equivalent in a browser is equally correct output.

competencies.statement: Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.

competencies.statement: Keep trusted renderer output distinct from untrusted author text and make every conversion into trusted markup explicit and reviewable.

competencies.statement: Map headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, links, images, tables, and landmarks to valid meaningful HTML with content constraints.

competencies.statement: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

misconceptions: Converting every image to the newest format is automatically faster and accessible.

misconceptions: A screen reader can infer the purpose of every “read more” link.

misconceptions: Pre and table elements are accessible without content-specific review.

misconceptions: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

competencies.statement: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.
misconceptions: Choosing common phone, tablet, and desktop widths makes a layout responsive.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.

competencies.statement: Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
misconceptions: A page that fits at 390 pixels will also work at zoom and with long content.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.

competencies.statement: Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.
misconceptions: A static site is progressively enhanced because its files are static.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.

competencies.statement: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.
misconceptions: One total transfer-size limit captures user-visible performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.

competencies.statement: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.
misconceptions: A local desktop load time predicts field performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

competencies.statement: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.
misconceptions: Static output cannot create serious security or privacy harm.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.

competencies.statement: Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
misconceptions: CommonMark-compliant raw HTML is safe because it came from a repository.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.

competencies.statement: Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.
misconceptions: Pathlib automatically prevents directory traversal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain content, include, template, output, and asset paths; reject traversal, symlink escape, special files, and output overlap.

competencies.statement: Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.
misconceptions: A private source repository keeps generated secrets private on public hosting.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat every output byte as public, scan for credentials and private metadata, minimize analytics, and require consent and retention policy.

competencies.statement: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.
misconceptions: A restrictive CSP repairs unsafe generated HTML.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.

competencies.statement: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
misconceptions: End-to-end site snapshots make lower-level tests redundant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.

competencies.statement: Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.
misconceptions: A large approved HTML snapshot clearly explains every intended behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.

competencies.statement: Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.
misconceptions: Property testing means generating random Markdown and expecting no exception.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.

competencies.statement: Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.
misconceptions: A parser that does not crash is safe against adversarial content.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.

competencies.statement: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.
misconceptions: Valid HTML proves routes, interactions, and reader tasks work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.

competencies.statement: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.
misconceptions: A stack trace is the most actionable build diagnostic for authors.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.

competencies.statement: Sort deterministically, deduplicate, cap cascades, promote configured warnings, summarize omissions, and return status from policy.
misconceptions: More warnings always produce more trustworthy builds.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Sort deterministically, deduplicate, cap cascades, promote configured warnings, summarize omissions, and return status from policy.

competencies.statement: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.
misconceptions: Total build time is enough observability for a generator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.

competencies.statement: Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.
misconceptions: The parser must be the bottleneck because every page uses it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.

competencies.statement: Redact secrets, home paths, private content, and identifiers; bound logs; define retention; and preserve enough identity for causal comparison.
misconceptions: Local build logs cannot expose sensitive information.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Redact secrets, home paths, private content, and identifiers; bound logs; define retention; and preserve enough identity for causal comparison.

competencies.statement: Declare build backend, project metadata, Python bounds, dependencies, optional groups, entry point, package data, license, and version source in pyproject.toml.
misconceptions: A requirements file fully describes an installable command.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare build backend, project metadata, Python bounds, dependencies, optional groups, entry point, package data, license, and version source in pyproject.toml.

competencies.statement: Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.
misconceptions: A console entry point receives command-line arguments automatically as parameters.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.

competencies.statement: Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.
misconceptions: Relative paths beside source modules work after wheel installation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.

competencies.statement: Define versioned capabilities, explicit enablement, discovery scope, configuration schema, isolation limits, deterministic order, and failure policy for plugins.
misconceptions: Python entry-point discovery makes third-party plugins trusted and compatible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define versioned capabilities, explicit enablement, discovery scope, configuration schema, isolation limits, deterministic order, and failure policy for plugins.

competencies.statement: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.
misconceptions: A successful editable install proves the release artifact works.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build sdist and wheel, inspect contents and metadata, install into fresh supported environments, run the command elsewhere, and verify uninstall boundaries.

competencies.statement: Eliminate unstable order, paths, locale, time, random values, permissions, and environment leakage; use recorded source and tool identity and compare clean digests.
misconceptions: A deterministic Python function guarantees a reproducible site artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Eliminate unstable order, paths, locale, time, random values, permissions, and environment leakage; use recorded source and tool identity and compare clean digests.

competencies.statement: Pin workflow dependencies, minimize permissions, isolate untrusted changes, protect environments, retain logs safely, and separate build from deployment authority.
misconceptions: A repository workflow is trusted because maintainers can review it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin workflow dependencies, minimize permissions, isolate untrusted changes, protect environments, retain logs safely, and separate build from deployment authority.

competencies.statement: Build once, validate, identify, attest, retain, approve, and deploy the exact same immutable artifact instead of rebuilding.
misconceptions: Re-running the build at deploy time is equivalent to artifact promotion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build once, validate, identify, attest, retain, approve, and deploy the exact same immutable artifact instead of rebuilding.

competencies.statement: Package the public root correctly, upload a Pages artifact, deploy only from an authorized event, and verify base path, custom domain, HTTPS, and not-found behavior.
misconceptions: Pushing generated files is the only way to deploy a custom generator to GitHub Pages.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Package the public root correctly, upload a Pages artifact, deploy only from an authorized event, and verify base path, custom domain, HTTPS, and not-found behavior.

competencies.statement: Verify representative URLs, headers, assets, fragments, accessibility tasks, canonical origin, cache behavior, artifact identity, and rollback trigger after publication.
misconceptions: A green deploy job proves the public site is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify representative URLs, headers, assets, fragments, accessibility tasks, canonical origin, cache behavior, artifact identity, and rollback trigger after publication.

competencies.statement: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
misconceptions: If every source file built, migration preserved the existing site.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.

competencies.statement: Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
misconceptions: Passing unit tests makes the latest artifact ready to publish.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.

competencies.statement: Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.
misconceptions: Redeploying yesterday’s source immediately restores every reader.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.

competencies.statement: Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.
misconceptions: Static sites need no incident or recovery plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose broken builds, partial deploys, missing routes, bad redirects, stale caches, expired domains, and compromised content with named owners and communication.

competencies.statement: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.
misconceptions: Thousands of generated steps or pages prove production readiness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend reader value, content authority, parser scope, route integrity, accessibility, security, performance, deployment, recovery, and residual risk together.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Production Static Site Generator with Python 3.14 Certificate
